claim: Graph representation and knowledge graphs are a way to extract signal from noise and express it in a knowledge-dense form; they help transform raw data into actionable knowledge.
actionable: When building knowledge systems from unstructured data: use graph structures to represent relationships and extract signal, not just flat documents.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: Graphs are the natural representation for enterprise data: networks, relationships, hierarchies (e.g., transport networks like the London tube are graphs; org structures are graphs).
actionable: For enterprise data systems: model relationships and hierarchies explicitly as graphs rather than flattening them into tables or documents.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: As of late 2025, there has been a dramatic shift from draft-generation (AI generating content for human review) to full automation (AI directly sends messages/emails to customers). This represents a tipping point in customer trust and system reliability.
actionable: Assess your AI product's readiness for customer-facing automation: what guarantees do you need for users to trust it to act autonomously?
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: Knowledge graphs are increasingly being used alongside RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) to provide structured, relationship-aware context to LLMs, rather than just flat document retrieval.
actionable: Augment your RAG systems with graph structure: retrieve not just documents but also relationships and context from a knowledge graph before passing to the LLM.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: Enterprise adoption of new platforms typically starts with a single application or project, not company-wide implementation; this is different from startups where the product is the company.
actionable: When selling to enterprises: plan for single-project pilots before enterprise-wide rollout; startups move faster with platform-level adoption.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: Document-based RAG retrieves flat text; Graph RAG retrieves structured relationships and context, enabling richer semantic understanding for LLMs, especially for complex queries requiring multi-hop reasoning.
actionable: For complex enterprise queries: implement Graph RAG to retrieve structured relationships alongside documents, enabling multi-hop reasoning instead of single-document lookup.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: The next generation of AI-native startups need graph capabilities built into their architectures from the start to stay competitive; this is becoming a core part of modern AI infrastructure.
actionable: For AI startups: evaluate whether your product needs structured relationship modeling; if yes, build graph capabilities early rather than bolting them on later.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J

claim: Using LLMs to extract structured entities and relationships from unstructured documents, then building a knowledge graph, is a practical way to turn documents into queryable knowledge.
actionable: Build a pipeline: LLM extracts entities/relationships from documents → store in graph → query graph for context-aware retrieval instead of document search.
source_title: ⚡️ How to turn Documents into Knowledge: Graphs in Modern AI — Emil Eifrem, CEO Neo4J
